steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Liu et al. concluded that these alloys' toughness differs from most HEAs and MEAs — meaning their toughness behaves unusually. The passage notes that typical HEAs and MEAs see toughness fall as temperature drops. A choice that shows the opposite pattern (toughness rising as temperature falls) would support the conclusion of unusual behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'CrCoNi outperformed CrMnFeCoNi at 20 K': Compares the two alloys against each other at one temperature point but does not describe how either behaves across temperatures to show unusual toughness behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — 'propagation toughness substantially higher than initiation toughness throughout': Contradicted by the graph: at 77 K, initiation and propagation values converge for both alloys, so 'substantially higher throughout' is false. ✗
  • Choice C — 'changes greater between 20 K and 77 K than between 77 K and 198 K': Contradicted for CrCoNi initiation toughness, where the drop from 77 K to 198 K exceeds the drop from 20 K to 77 K. ✗
  • Choice D — 'initiation and propagation toughness lower at 293 K than at 20 K for both alloys': Shows that both alloys' toughness is higher at cryogenic temperatures than at room temperature — the opposite of the typical HEA/MEA pattern, directly supporting the conclusion of unusual behavior. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
